title: Grastidium haemoglossum
taxonomy: Grastidium haemoglossum is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It is native to Australia and is classified in the genus Grastidium. The plant belongs to the family Poaceae, which includes many types of grasses. Grastidium haemoglossum is further classified as a member of the subfamily Danthonioideae and the tribe Danthonieae. Within the Poaceae family, Grastidium haemoglossum is also classified as a monocot.

properties: Grastidium haemoglossum, a species of moss, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. It is believed to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which may contribute to its medicinal value. The moss is thought to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions.
